おやすみなさい.meaning: good night (polite phrase used before bed)

ございます.meaning: polite verb suffix; elevates phrases like 'ohayou' or 'arigatou' to formal speech

どういたしまして.meaning: you're welcome (standard response to thank you)

おねがいします.meaning: please; used when making requests, asking for favors, or ordering items politely

すみません.meaning: excuse me / I'm sorry / thank you (all-purpose polite phrase for attention or minor apology)

おげんき.meaning: healthy / well (the polite prefix 'o-' attached to 'genki')

ですか.meaning: is it? (the copula 'desu' combined with the question marker particle 'ka')

の.meaning: possessive or modifying particle; links nouns to show ownership or association (like 's in English or 'of')

は.meaning: topic marker particle (pronounced 'wa'); highlights what the main focus of the sentence is

はじめまして.meaning: nice to meet you (used exclusively the very first time you meet someone)

どうぞ.meaning: please / here you go / after you (used when offering an item or permission to someone)

よろしく.meaning: kindly / best regards / please favor me (expresses hope for a good future relationship)

おつかれさまです.meaning: thank you for your hard work (standard phrase to acknowledge colleague effort)

いただきます.meaning: let's eat (humble expression of gratitude used strictly before starting a meal)

ごちそうさまでした.meaning: thank you for the meal (polite phrase used after finishing eating)

いってきます.meaning: I'm leaving (said by a person leaving their home/office, meaning 'I'll go and come back')

いってらっしゃい.meaning: please go and come back safely (said to the person who is leaving)

おかえりなさい.meaning: welcome back home (polite response to someone returning)

わかりました.meaning: I understood (past tense, polite form of the verb 'wakaru')

わかりません.meaning: I don't understand / I don't know (negative, polite form of the verb 'wakaru')

しっています.meaning: I know / I am aware (continuous polite state of the verb 'shiru')

しりません.meaning: I don't know / I am unfamiliar with (negative polite form of the verb 'shiru')

はなしてください.meaning: please speak (the -te request form of 'hanasu' followed by 'kudasai')

あれ.meaning: that over there (object far from both speaker and listener)

いくら.meaning: how much (used primarily to ask for financial cost or price)

どこに.meaning: where to / at where (combines 'doko' [where] with the destination/location particle 'ni')

いきますか.meaning: are you going? (polite present form of 'iku' [to go] + question particle 'ka')

が.meaning: subject marker particle; identifies the specific noun performing the action or experiencing the state

あきました.meaning: became empty (past tense polite form of 'aku'; used in phrases to mean 'became hungry')

かわきました.meaning: became dry (past tense polite form of 'kawaku'; used in phrases to mean 'became thirsty')

を.meaning: direct object marker particle (pronounced 'o'); marks the noun directly affected by a verb

ください.meaning: please give me (used when requesting an item directly)

ひとつ.meaning: one item (generic native Japanese item counter)

みず.meaning: water (specifically cold or room temperature drinking water)

おかいけい.meaning: the bill / restaurant check (polite noun structure)

ちかくの.meaning: nearby / of the vicinity (combines 'chikaku' [near] + modifier particle 'no')

かいたい.meaning: want to buy (the desire conjugation suffix '-tai' added to the root of 'kau' [to buy])

たかい.meaning: expensive / costly / physically high or tall (i-adjective)

つかえますか.meaning: can it be used? (potential verb form 'tsukaeru' [can use] + question particle 'ka')

りょうしゅうしょ.meaning: receipt / formal proof of business payment

つかれました.meaning: got tired / became exhausted (past tense polite form of 'tsukaredu')

あつい.meaning: hot (used exclusively for climate/weather temperature, i-adjective)

すきです.meaning: like / love (na-adjective descriptor 'suki' + polite asserting copula 'desu')

いきましょう.meaning: let's go (the suggestive/volitional polite ending '-mashou' applied to 'iku')

きをつけて.meaning: be careful / take care (te-form of the phrase 'ki o tsukeru')

がんばって.meaning: good luck / do your best / hang in there (te-form of 'ganbaru', implies active effort)

たすけて.meaning: help me! (te-form of the verb 'tasukeru' [to rescue/help])

よんで.meaning: please call / summon (te-form of the verb 'yobu'; invites an implied 'kudasai')
